title: The Treaty
weakest_section: 05:00-05:30 range with the racist job applicant

jokes.text: Oh hey, Ben. Have you seen my complete collection of all 193 national flags? Ooh! Here they are.
jokes.setup: Leslie asks if Ben has seen her flag collection
jokes.punchline: She immediately finds them herself, making the question pointless
jokes.explanation: Perfect Leslie moment - her obsessive organization and immediate self-sufficiency. Well-structured quick gag.

jokes.text: I kinda want to roll up my sleeves and make geopolitical problem-solving my bitch.
jokes.setup: Leslie discussing Model UN strategy seriously
jokes.punchline: Uses crude slang in academic context
jokes.explanation: Great character moment - Leslie's intense earnestness mixed with unexpected profanity. Very quotable.

jokes.text: Andy, will you be Iceland? / The bad guys from Mighty Ducks 2?
jokes.setup: Leslie asks Andy to represent Iceland in Model UN
jokes.punchline: Andy only knows countries from movies where they're villains
jokes.explanation: Brilliant Andy moment - his pop culture lens on world geography is perfectly in character and genuinely funny.

jokes.text: How 'bout Germany? / They've never been the bad guys.
jokes.setup: Leslie suggests Germany after Andy rejects other countries for being movie villains
jokes.punchline: Andy's obliviousness to Germany's actual historical role as 'bad guys'
jokes.explanation: Dark humor works because it's filtered through Andy's innocent ignorance. Economical and sharp.

jokes.text: And I'll be the Moon. / No, you're gonna be South Africa or Pakistan. / I'm the Moon, or I quit.
jokes.setup: Andy wants to be the Moon in Model UN
jokes.punchline: His absolute commitment to this impossible choice
jokes.explanation: Absurdist perfection - Andy's childlike logic and ultimatum is both ridiculous and oddly admirable.

jokes.text: Fine. You be South Africa, and you can also secretly run the moon. / The Moon accepts your ridiculous proposal.
jokes.setup: Leslie tries to compromise on Andy's Moon demand
jokes.punchline: Andy speaks formally as 'The Moon' accepting the deal
jokes.explanation: Great payoff to the Moon setup. Andy's diplomatic language as 'The Moon' is absurdly perfect.

jokes.text: Look! I'll let you be America. / And teach kids that not only is government good but that there should be a World-wide super-government? I'd rather sand down my toenails.
jokes.setup: Leslie offers Ron the choice role of America
jokes.punchline: Ron's libertarian horror at the concept plus bizarre personal detail
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron moment - his anti-government stance mixed with oddly specific personal detail about toenails.

jokes.text: Every three weeks, I have to sand down my toenails. They're too strong for clippers.
jokes.setup: Ron's confessional about his toenail maintenance
jokes.punchline: The image of Ron's superhuman toenails requiring power tools
jokes.explanation: Iconic Ron moment - combines his manliness with absurd specificity. Highly quotable and memorable.

jokes.text: I really wouldn't describe myself as a Model U.N. legend. I'd go with icon. Or hero.
jokes.setup: Leslie modestly downplays being called a Model UN legend
jokes.punchline: She suggests even more grandiose titles
jokes.explanation: Classic Leslie misdirection - appears humble then reveals even greater ego. Well-structured.

jokes.text: Look, if you ask me, Enron is down but not out. Who doesn't like a comeback story?
jokes.setup: Tom casually discussing business prospects
jokes.punchline: His tone-deaf suggestion that the criminal corporation Enron could make a comeback
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about corporate scandal. Shows Tom's business naivety and poor judgment calls.

jokes.text: Then two days ago, I saw him spraying cologne samples at Macy's.
jokes.setup: Ron describing Tom's supposed success as an entrepreneur
jokes.punchline: The reality of Tom working a department store sample job
jokes.explanation: Good contrast between Tom's claims and reality. Visual of Tom in this job is inherently funny.

jokes.text: You were grinding with her pretty hard at April's Halloween party.
jokes.setup: Chris describing his romantic connection with Jerry's daughter
jokes.punchline: Ann's blunt description of his dancing
jokes.explanation: Competent cringe humor. Ann's directness contrasts with Chris's euphemisms.

jokes.text: Wow. A four-date-long relationship might not be 100% perfect? I think you should launch a full-scale investigation.
jokes.setup: Chris treating a minor dating hiccup as a major crisis
jokes.punchline: Ann's sarcastic overreaction suggestion
jokes.explanation: Solid sarcastic response to Chris's overthinking. Good character dynamic.

jokes.text: The root of sarcasm is truth, Ann. I am going to launch a full-scale investigation.
jokes.setup: Ann sarcastically suggests an investigation
jokes.punchline: Chris completely misses the sarcasm and takes it seriously
jokes.explanation: Perfect Chris moment - his earnestness making him immune to sarcasm. Great character consistency.

jokes.text: But Ben and I have so much in common. I mean, we're amazing friends. And friendship is better because friends help you move. They drive you to the airport. Boyfriends just... love you and marry you.
jokes.setup: Leslie explaining why friendship with Ben is better than romance
jokes.punchline: Her dismissive description of romantic love as just 'love you and marry you'
jokes.explanation: Great insight into Leslie's denial. The casual dismissal of love and marriage is both funny and revealing.

jokes.text: You're too accessible. Every time she calls, you answer. You gotta dangle the carrot, literally and metaphorically.
jokes.setup: Chris asking for dating advice
jokes.punchline: Donna's advice about dangling carrots both ways
jokes.explanation: Decent Donna moment with sexual innuendo. The literal/metaphorical distinction is mildly clever.

jokes.text: No, no, no. I was fired because I made a pass at an ethnic woman.
jokes.setup: Job applicant being asked about his 1968 retirement
jokes.punchline: His casual admission of sexual harassment with racist language
jokes.explanation: Dark humor about workplace harassment. Effective cringe but relies on offensive content for shock value.

jokes.text: Oh, you know, hitchhiking around. I went to Mexico. I met a lot of ethnic girls. They grow on trees down there.
jokes.setup: Continuing his job interview story
jokes.punchline: His racist and absurd 'grow on trees' comment
jokes.explanation: Lowest craft score for relying on racist stereotypes for humor. The 'grow on trees' metaphor is absurdist but offensive.

jokes.text: He said he liked ethnic girls, Tom.
jokes.setup: Tom calling the applicant racist
jokes.punchline: Ron's oblivious defense focusing on the wrong part
jokes.explanation: Good Ron moment - his complete missing of the point is perfectly in character and well-delivered.

jokes.text: I was the first person to abbreviate Parks and Recreation Department. First it was Parks and Recreation. Then Parks and Rec. Then P&R. Then lengthened it just a little bit to... Tommy's place.
jokes.setup: Tom describing his innovations at work
jokes.punchline: His 'abbreviation' that ends up being longer and completely different
jokes.explanation: Excellent Tom moment showing his narcissism and illogical thinking. The escalation to 'Tommy's place' is perfect.

jokes.text: Under-exaggerate much, France? Ha ha! Nice!
jokes.setup: Leslie making a political comment in character as Denmark
jokes.punchline: Her made-up word 'under-exaggerate' and self-congratulation
jokes.explanation: Good Leslie moment showing her competitive nature and made-up vocabulary. Self-congratulation is in character.

jokes.text: Leslie and I aren't dating anymore, but, uh, we're friends. So it's fun. It's just fun. It's fun... it's... fun... It is fun.
jokes.setup: Ben explaining his relationship status to camera
jokes.punchline: His increasingly desperate repetition of 'fun' revealing his discomfort
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Ben's discomfort. The repetition effectively builds the awkwardness.

jokes.text: Honey! I just traded Finland's military to Kenya for 50 lions. That's pretty good, right?
jokes.setup: Andy excitedly reporting his Model UN diplomacy
jokes.punchline: The absurd trade of military for lions
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy logic - treating Model UN like a video game. The military-for-lions trade is beautifully absurd.

jokes.text: But also militaries are pretty good at protecting countries. / But so are lions. And you don't have to pay them.
jokes.setup: April pointing out the flaw in Andy's lion strategy
jokes.punchline: Andy's counterargument about lions being free protection
jokes.explanation: Brilliant absurdist logic from Andy. His economic argument for lions as military is perfectly stupid and confident.

jokes.text: I definitely have more lions than any other country in the whole world right now. I have no idea what's going on. But if that ends up meaning something in this game, I'd say I'm set.
jokes.setup: Andy's confessional about his Model UN strategy
jokes.punchline: His confidence despite complete ignorance, based solely on lion accumulation
jokes.explanation: Great callback that builds on the lion trade. Andy's confidence despite confusion is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Well, how about me make it a cool 40?
jokes.setup: Ben asking Leslie to wrap up her photo op in 10-15 minutes
jokes.punchline: Leslie casually escalating to 40 minutes
jokes.explanation: Good Leslie moment showing her obliviousness to others' needs. The casual escalation is in character.

jokes.text: Shut up, Bulgaria!
jokes.setup: A student commenting on Leslie's awesome life
jokes.punchline: Ben's frustrated outburst at a teenager
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment showing Ben's frustration. Yelling at a student is both inappropriate and relatable.

jokes.text: Or, as they say in Denmark... 'I'm back.' Most people speak English.
jokes.setup: Leslie trying to say something in Danish character
jokes.punchline: Ben's dry observation that she's just speaking English
jokes.explanation: Nice setup-punchline with Ben's deadpan delivery. Leslie's fake foreign language attempt is well-deflated.

jokes.text: The nation of Denmark would like to formerly request a summit with the nation of Peru over there by the refreshment table. Because the nation of Denmark needs a juice box.
jokes.setup: Leslie speaking in formal diplomatic language
jokes.punchline: The childish reason for the formal diplomatic request
jokes.explanation: Good juxtaposition between formal diplomacy and childish needs. The juice box is a perfect deflating detail.

jokes.text: You can't just chop up the aspects of a relationship into discrete parts and select the ones you want like a buffet. / Why not?
jokes.setup: Ben explaining why friendship after romance doesn't work
jokes.punchline: Leslie's genuine confusion about why this is a problem
jokes.explanation: Good relationship humor. Leslie's compartmentalization and genuine confusion is perfectly in character.

jokes.text: Due to a recent betrayal, my homeland, the great state of Denmark, has officially decided to declare war on Peru. Scandinavian brothers, on my signal. Unleash hell!
jokes.setup: Leslie getting increasingly upset about the Model UN situation
jokes.punchline: Her dramatic declaration of war in a high school exercise
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation moment. Leslie's dramatic overreaction to personal conflict is both absurd and in character.

jokes.text: No. I'm not kidding. My name's Gary, and I'm from Gary, Indiana. Amazing. People like hearing that. It's probably one of my stronger anecdotes.
jokes.setup: Job applicant sharing what he thinks is an interesting fact
jokes.punchline: His complete misunderstanding of what makes a good anecdote
jokes.explanation: Good cringe humor about boring people who don't know they're boring. The confidence is perfectly misplaced.

jokes.text: A fact is not an anecdote, Gary. Here's an anecdote... Today I met the most boring man in the world. His name was Gary.
jokes.setup: Tom correcting Gary about anecdotes
jokes.punchline: Tom's brutal example using Gary himself
jokes.explanation: Excellent burn from Tom. Perfect structure with the definition followed by the brutal example. Very quotable.

jokes.text: Under sex, you wrote... 'Yes. Ha ha, ha ha, ha ha.' Well-played.
jokes.setup: Tom reviewing Keith's job application
jokes.punchline: Keith's juvenile response to a standard form question
jokes.explanation: Basic juvenile humor. The 'well-played' response from Tom elevates it slightly but it's still fairly predictable.

jokes.text: Like one... one month of money... Would be... would be just right.
jokes.setup: Keith being asked about his career goals
jokes.punchline: His extremely limited and honest ambition
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment. Keith's honesty about wanting minimal commitment is both refreshing and terrible.

jokes.text: the only thing I will be waving is your decapitated head on a stick in front of your weeping mother!
jokes.setup: Ben suggesting Leslie should wave a white flag
jokes.punchline: Leslie's extremely violent counter-threat
jokes.explanation: Excellent escalation from Leslie. The graphic violence from the usually cheerful character is shocking and memorable.

jokes.text: Competent enough to keep the bosses off my back, but selfish enough to slow down all the work. Tom Haverfords don't grow on trees. If they did, I'd sell 'em. Tommy trees.
jokes.setup: Ron describing why Tom was perfect for the job
jokes.punchline: His business idea for 'Tommy trees'
jokes.explanation: Perfect Ron logic - his backhanded compliment and immediate capitalist instinct. Very quotable.

jokes.text: I'm like a shark. I don't swim backwards.
jokes.setup: Tom explaining why he can't return to his old job
jokes.punchline: His pseudo-profound shark metaphor
jokes.explanation: Good Tom moment with his typical pseudo-business wisdom. The shark metaphor is appropriately ridiculous.

jokes.text: Yeah! We got the freaking moon. What are you gonna do without tides, Peru?
jokes.setup: The Moon joining Leslie's coalition
jokes.punchline: Andy's surprisingly scientific threat about tides
jokes.explanation: Brilliant callback to Andy being the Moon, with surprisingly scientific knowledge. The tides threat is both stupid and clever.

jokes.text: And the jacuzzi broke. But I did... eat food off of your body.
jokes.setup: Ann describing a romantic getaway with Chris
jokes.punchline: Her casual mention of body food in front of coworkers
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment with Ann oversharing. The casual delivery makes it funnier.

jokes.text: 'Cause you guys suck. Look around. We thought you were gonna teach us how to solve problems. You've ruined my favorite club. Thanks a lot.
jokes.setup: The Model UN falling apart due to Leslie and Ben's conflict
jokes.punchline: Student's brutal but accurate assessment
jokes.explanation: Good reality check moment. The student's directness cuts through the adults' nonsense effectively.

jokes.text: When Ben gets upset, he becomes a really bad roommate. He takes really long sadness baths and makes me late for stuff.
jokes.setup: April explaining why Leslie should talk to Ben
jokes.punchline: The image of Ben taking 'sadness baths'
jokes.explanation: Excellent character moment. 'Sadness baths' is perfectly specific and pathetic for Ben. Very quotable.

jokes.text: you basically turned me into a female version of yourself, and then you got bored dating me because you were dating yourself.
jokes.setup: Ann explaining what went wrong with Chris
jokes.punchline: Her insight about Chris dating a version of himself
jokes.explanation: Smart relationship observation. Ann's analysis of Chris's narcissistic dating pattern is insightful and funny.

jokes.text: Jogging is the worst, Chris. I mean, I know it keeps you healthy, but God, at what cost?
jokes.setup: Ann describing how Chris changed her
jokes.punchline: Her dramatic assessment of jogging's personal cost
jokes.explanation: Relatable observation about exercise. The dramatic 'at what cost?' makes it funnier than it should be.

jokes.text: If you rearrange the letters of Peru, you could spell Europe. / That's... That's not true. / Well... You have to rearrange them.
jokes.setup: Andy making a diplomatic observation
jokes.punchline: His insistence on his wrong anagram despite correction
jokes.explanation: Perfect Andy logic. His confidence in his completely wrong anagram and doubling down is beautifully absurd.

jokes.text: How do you know about Camp David? / How do you know about camp David?
jokes.setup: Leslie confused about Andy knowing diplomatic history
jokes.punchline: Revealing Andy was talking about his friend's backyard
jokes.explanation: Good misdirection moment. The parallel between diplomatic Camp David and backyard 'camp David' is clever.

jokes.text: Ben, how are we gonna get Kathy Ireland to do naked aerobics for them? / Okay. I regret telling you that... very much.
jokes.setup: Ben suggesting something that would appeal to teenage boys
jokes.punchline: Leslie's specific interpretation and Ben's horror
jokes.explanation: Good cringe moment about Ben's teenage fantasy being exposed. Leslie's literalness makes it worse for Ben.

jokes.text: There's a Macy's in town? Where's the Macy's in town? I heard they have an amazing cologne sample guy.
jokes.setup: Ron catching Tom at his Macy's job
jokes.punchline: Tom's continued denial while praising his own work
jokes.explanation: Good callback with Tom's denial and inadvertent self-praise. His pride in his cologne sampling is perfectly Tom.

jokes.text: The nicest room in City Hall by a factor of like a million.
jokes.setup: Leslie showing off the council chambers
jokes.punchline: Her mathematical hyperbole about the room's quality
jokes.explanation: Decent Leslie enthusiasm moment. The mathematical hyperbole is in character but not particularly clever.

jokes.text: Passion is what makes for good treaties... Good relationships... Good pizza. Good friends. Good everything. Including wars.
jokes.setup: Leslie waxing philosophical about passion
jokes.punchline: Including wars in her list of things improved by passion
jokes.explanation: Good escalation with Leslie's earnest philosophy accidentally including war. The list format builds nicely to the dark turn.

jokes.text: Sorry. These are really important.
jokes.setup: Jerry complaining about noise from the copier
jokes.punchline: Tom dismissing Jerry's comfort for his unimportant copying
jokes.explanation: Basic Tom moment showing his disregard for Jerry. Functional but not particularly clever.
